violations.text: Some of the individual cutting boards are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment has employees that are certified in food safety. An employee must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as a Certified Food Safety Manager. New Violation. Correct By: 10/15/2024

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 10/04/2024

Steak for steak sandwiches was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Steak was reheated to 186 degrees F and returned to hot hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2024

A small section of the floor and wall junctures under the prep sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024

A small section of the floor and wall junctures under the prep sink do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. In addition, there is a hole in the floor surrounding a pipe under the prep sink. This hole is large enough for pest entry. Coving and floor must be repaired using proper materials. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine and nozzles of milk dispensers in the cafeteria have an accumulation of food residue and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

A section of the floor and wall junctures behind/under the prep sink do not have coving and a small opening observed in the floor. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2023
